expectations.text: Reads and analyzes the PRD document
expectations.evidence: Step 2a explicitly describes reading and parsing the PRD with a three-pass strategy (structure recognition, information extraction, gap analysis). Extracts vision, users, features, constraints, dependencies, unknowns from docs/prds/billing-v2.md.

Feature Classification table uses P0-P2 with rationale for each: Subscription Management (P0, core capability), Metered Billing (P0, core value proposition), Reporting/Analytics (P2, nice to have). Billing Operations epic set to P1. Uses MoSCoW priority matrix from prd-analysis.md.

Full hierarchy created: 6 epics, 10 stories, 14 tasks + 2 spikes. All tasks have hour estimates (2-8h range). Stories have Given/When/Then acceptance criteria. Epics have descriptions and priorities. MCP calls shown for create_epic, create_story, create_task, add_dependency.

Skill routing table maps features to skills: database-design for schema/migrations, api-design for all API endpoints, security-audit implicit for tenant isolation. Each task in the JSON plan has a 'skill' field. Skill Assignments section in PROJECT.md summarizes routing.

Third Pass (Gap Analysis) identifies missing items: tax jurisdiction handling, dunning/retry policy details, revenue recognition requirements. Assumptions documented (single currency, Stripe, PostgreSQL). Open Questions Flagged section at end lists tax calculation, currency handling, accounting integration. Two spikes created for high-uncertainty items.

Full .claude/PROJECT.md generated with project overview, status, metrics table (6 epics, 10 stories, 14 tasks, 2 spikes, ~78 hours), tech stack, feature overview table, skill assignments, critical path, current gaps, next actions, key files, and session log.
